name: cm-cluster-on-demand-aws

metadata.name: cm-cluster-on-demand-aws
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Bright Cluster on Demand Utility AWS

documentation:
With Cluster on Demand for AWS you can spin up a cluster running Bright Cluster
Manager inside of AWS.

# Installation

```
pip install cm-cluster-on-demand-aws
```

# Usage

To get started, execute the following command:

```
cm-cod-aws --help
```

Example, To start a Bright Cluster with 5 nodes and 1 headnode in AWS:

```
cm-cod-aws cluster create --on-error 'cleanup' --aws-region 'us-east-1' --wlm 'slurm' --nodes '5' --aws-access-key-id '...' --aws-secret-key '...' --cluster-password '...'  --license-product-key '...' --name mycluster
```

Don't forget to fill in the '...' blanks, and change the other parameters to
the values that match your use-case and organization. To obtain a Bright
product key, please contact sales@brightcomputing.com. All documentation for
setting up and running a Bright cluster using Cluster On Demand on AWS can be
found in the
[Cloudbursting Manual](https://www.brightcomputing.com/documentation).

If you have an account in Bright Computing's Customer Portal, you can also
create a AWS/Azure cluster from there.

classes.name: clusterondemandaws.base.ClusterCommandBase
documentation:
Base class for all AWS cluster commands.

This class only contains non-public validator methods that are intended to be used by
descendant classes to validate user input. The general contract for all these methods is
to perform various input sanitization checks, raising an Exception in the case of a failed
check. If the check passes the _validate_xxx methods will simply return control to the
caller with no return value.

documentation: Create a cluster in a pre-existing (user-managed) VPC

functions.name: clusterondemandaws.vpc.create_vpc
documentation:
Create an AWS VPC with the given name.

This VPC contains a single head node, two subnets, an internal gateway and one routing table.

functions.name: clusterondemandaws.vpc._set_default_gateway_subnet
documentation: When the default gateway of the subnet is an ec2 instance the ec2 instance must be in
the running state. Even though we make sure to check that an instance is running before
we set the gateway, we can still encounter incorrect instance state errors.

functions.name: clusterondemandaws.vpc.get_aws_tag
documentation:
For some mysterious reason, boto3 stores tags as a list of dictionaries:
[{'Key': 'Name', 'Value': 'my-value-foo'}].
This function retrieves the value

classes.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster._ResourceProtocol
documentation: Helper class for convenient and safe usage of resources with common properties.

methods.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster.Cluster.map_head_node_type_to_address
documentation:
This function extracts private_ip_addresses from an instance object and determines which private_ip_address is
A, B or HA. private_ip_addresses is a list of: [private_ip_address, private_ip_address]

```
param

instance: boto3.resources.factory.ec2.Instance

return

{self.IpType.A: {private_ip_address}, ...}
```

methods.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster.Cluster.allocate_and_associate_head_node_eips
documentation:
Sometimes the instance does not have a public EIP attached to a private ip address (E.g. creating or starting),
This function will allocate an EIP, then associate it for every private ip address of the instance.
If the interface already has public IP, it will be returned, instead of (re)attaching a new one.

```
param

instance: boto3.resources.factory.ec2.Instance

return

private_ip_addresses: [(ip_type, allocation_id, allocation_ip)]
```

methods.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster.Cluster.disassociate_and_release_head_node_eips
documentation:
If public EIP is attached to the private IP address of the instance, disassociate and then release it. Used when
stopping or terminating the instance
:param: instance: boto3.resources.factory.ec2.Instance
:return:

methods.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster.Cluster.stop
documentation:
We will try to stop the instance only if it's in "running" or "pending" state.
Instance states: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_InstanceState.html

methods.name: clusterondemandaws.cluster.Cluster.start
documentation:
We will try to start the instance only if it's not in "running" or "pending" state.
Instance states: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_InstanceState.html
